keyPoints: Deliberate desirable-difficulty prompts embedded in AI-assisted learning (reasoning explanations, misstep anticipation, partial-solution generation).
Long-run transfer measured with novel-context tasks and 4–8 week delays, plus PERMA-like engagement metrics.
Multi-arm RCT designs to parse depth-prompts, spacing/interleaving, and retrieval components.
Domain breadth to test domain-general transfer across physics, math, language, and beyond.
Rigorous governance and QA to mitigate AI hallucinations and ensure instructional alignment.
Actionable design templates: prompt taxonomy, prompt templates, spacing/interleaving schedules, and evaluation plans.
Explicit articulation of failure modes (homogenization, over-trust) and countermeasures (deliberate difficulty, diverse outputs, reflective prompts).
